import sys

def main():
    input_data = sys.stdin.readline().split()
    n, k = int(input_data[0]), int(input_data[1])
    
    or_vals = [0] * (n + 1)
    and_vals = [0] * (n + 1)
    
    for j in range(2, n + 1):
        print(f"or 1 {j}")
        sys.stdout.flush()
        or_vals[j] = int(sys.stdin.readline())
        print(f"and 1 {j}")
        sys.stdout.flush()
        and_vals[j] = int(sys.stdin.readline())
    
    # a[j] bits only in a[j] (not a[1]): or_vals[j] & ~a[1]
    # a[1] bits only in a[1] (not a[j]): or_vals[j] & ~a[j]
    # shared: and_vals[j]
    # Try a[1] = and_vals[2] | (or_vals[2] & ~(and_vals[2] | (or_vals[2] & ~or_vals[3])))
    # Just reconstruct: a[j] given a[1]: a[j] = and_vals[j] | (or_vals[j] & ~a1)
    # Find a[1]: it must satisfy a[1] & a[j] = and_vals[j] and a[1] | a[j] = or_vals[j]
    # a[1] = (or_vals[2] & ~(or_vals[2] & ~and_vals[2] & ~and_vals[3] & or_vals[3]))  -- complex
    # Simply: a[1] can be any value where (a1 & a[j]=and_vals[j]) and (a1|a[j]=or_vals[j])
    # a[1] must have all bits in and_vals[j], no bits outside or_vals[j]
    # bits in or_vals[j] but not and_vals[j] go to either a[1] or a[j]
    # Use two different j's to pin down a[1]
    
    # bits of a[1]: must be in or_vals[j] for all j, and include and_vals[j] for all j
    # a[1] = (intersection of or_vals) with some subset of ambiguous bits, plus (union of and_vals)
    # ambiguous bits for j: or_vals[j] & ~and_vals[j]  -- exactly one of a[1],a[j] has each
    # a[1]'s ambiguous bits for j2 must be consistent
    
    # a[1] has bit b iff: b in and_vals[j] for some j (then a[1] definitely has it)
    #                  or b in or_vals[j] & ~and_vals[j] and a[1] has it (not a[j])
    
    # Use j=2 and j=3:

    # ambiguous_3 = or_vals[3] & ~and_vals[3]
    # For bit b in ambiguous_2 & ambiguous_3: a[1] has b, or (a[2] has b AND a[3] has b)

    # So: if b in ambiguous_2 and b NOT in or_vals[3]: b not in a[1], b in a[2]

    sys.stdout.flush()
